If the Minister decides that subsection (2) does not apply in respect of a project, the Minister shall, without delay, provide notice of the decision to the proponent of the project and any federal authority that is likely to exercise a power or perform a duty or function referred to in section 5 in respect of the project.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 11:

Section.MarginalNote: Exercise and performance by Agency of certain responsible authority’s powers, duties and functions

Subsection.Text: Despite any other provision of this Act, if the Agency considers that a project for which it has received information is described in the comprehensive study list and that an environmental assessment of the project might be required and, if the Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission established under section 8 of the Nuclear Safety and Control Act or the National Energy Board established under section 3 of the National Energy Board Act is not one of the responsible authorities in relation to the project, the Agency shall commence a comprehensive study of the project and exercise the powers and perform the duties and functions of the responsible authority in relation to the project under this Act until the Minister is provided with the comprehensive study report as required by section 21.3.
When the Agency commences a comprehensive study of a project, it shall, without delay, send a notice to any responsible authority in relation to that project.

When, in relation to a project, the Agency exercises the powers and performs the duties and functions of the responsible authority under subsection (1), it is also required to exercise the powers and perform the duties and functions of the responsible authority under subsection 79(1) of the Species at Risk Act and, with respect to identifying the adverse effects of the project on a listed wildlife species and its critical habitat, is required to exercise the powers and perform the duties and functions of the responsible authority under subsection 79(2) of that Act.

Any federal authority that receives information from a proponent in relation to a project for which, in the federal authority’s opinion, a comprehensive study might be required, shall forward, without delay, that information to the Agency.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 15:

Section.MarginalNote: Minister’s power to establish scope of project

Subsection.Text: Despite section 15, the Minister may, if the conditions that the Minister establishes are met, determine that the scope of the project in relation to which an environmental assessment is to be conducted is limited to one or more components of that project.
The conditions referred to in subsection (1) must be made available to the public.

The Minister may, in writing and subject to any conditions that the Minister may specify, delegate to a responsible authority in relation to a project the power conferred on the Minister by subsection (1) in respect of that project.
The delegation may be in respect of a project or a class of projects.

Section.Text: Sections 21 to 21.2 of the Act are replaced by the following:
If a project is described in the comprehensive study list, the responsible authority shall ensure that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a comprehensive study of the project is conducted; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a comprehensive study report is prepared.

Subsection.Text: Within 10 days after the inclusion on the Internet site of a notice of commencement of the comprehensive study, the responsible authority shall give a notice, in any manner that it considers appropriate, providing the public with an opportunity to comment on the project and the conduct of the comprehensive study.
The notice must include the deadline and address for filing comments.

Section.Text: The responsible authority shall ensure that the public is provided with an opportunity, in addition to the ones provided under subsection 21.1(1) and section 22, to participate in the comprehensive study, subject to a determination that is made by the federal environmental assessment coordinator under paragraph 12.3(c) regarding the timing of the participation.
The responsible authority shall ensure that the Minister and the Agency are provided with the comprehensive study report.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 23(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: notice of the commencement of the comprehensive study;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 23(3)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a description of the factors to be taken into consideration in the environmental assessment and of the scope of those factors or an indication of how such a description may be obtained; and

Section.Text: Section 26 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 26(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Termination of comprehensive study by Agency
Subsection.Text: When it conducts a comprehensive study of a project, the Agency may, at any time, terminate the comprehensive study if all federal authorities decide not to exercise any power or perform any duty or function referred to in section 5 in relation to that project.
Paragraph 55.1(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a description of the scope of the project in relation to which an environmental assessment is to be conducted, as determined under section 15 or 15.1;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 55.1(2)(i) and (j) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: if the responsible authority, in accordance with subsection 18(3), gives the public an opportunity to participate in the screening of a project or when a comprehensive study is conducted, a description of the factors to be taken into consideration in the environmental assessment and of the scope of those factors or an indication of how such a description may be obtained;

Section.Text: Subsection 55.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Agency shall ensure that the records referred to in paragraphs 55.1(2)(b), (e) and (l) are included in the Internet site.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding, after section 82, the schedule set out in Schedule 3 to this Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Non-application of amended provisions to certain comprehensive studies
Section.Text: Any comprehensive study of a project commenced under the Canadian Environmental Assessment Act before the day on which this section comes into force must be continued and completed as if this Act had not come into force if, before that day, the Minister referred the project to the responsible authority to continue the comprehensive study.

With respect to a comprehensive study of a project commenced under the Canadian Environmental Assessment Act before the day on which this section comes into force and in relation to which section 2162 does not apply, the period referred to in section 21.1 of that Act, as enacted by section 2156, is deemed to commence on that day.
The definitions Building Canada Plan, environmentally sensitive area and intelligent transportation system in subsection 1(1) of the Exclusion List Regulations, 2007 are repealed.
Section 5 of the Regulations is repealed.
Schedule 4 to the Regulations is repealed.

Heading.TitleText: Regulations Amending the Exclusion List Regulations, 2007

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Regulations Amending the Exclusion List Regulations, 2007 is repealed.
Section 4 of the Regulations is repealed.
Section 6 of the Regulations is repealed.
Subsection 7(2) of the Regulations is repealed.

Heading.TitleText: Infrastructure Projects Environmental Assessment Adaptation Regulations

Section.Text: The Infrastructure Projects Environmental Assessment Adaptation Regulations are repealed.
The definition appeals officer in subsection 122(1) of the Canada Labour Code is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: appeals officer means a person who is appointed as an appeals officer under section 145.1;

Section.Text: Subsection 129(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a health and safety officer decides that the danger does not exist, the employee is not entitled under section 128 or this section to continue to refuse to use or operate the machine or thing, work in that place or perform that activity, but the employee, or a person designated by the employee for the purpose, may appeal the decision by filing a notice of appeal with the Minister within 10 days after the day on which the employee receives notice of the decision.

Section.Text: Subsection 145.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On receipt of a notice of appeal, the Minister shall appoint as an appeals officer to inquire into and make a decision on the appeal any person who is qualified to perform the duties of such an officer.

Section.Text: Subsection 146(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An employer, employee or trade union that feels aggrieved by a direction issued by a health and safety officer under this Part may appeal the direction by filing a notice of appeal with the Minister within 30 days after the day on which the direction is issued or confirmed in writing.
The portion of subsection 146.1(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If an appeal is brought under subsection 129(7) or section 146, the appeals officer shall, in a summary way and without delay, inquire into the circumstances of the decision or direction, as the case may be, and may
Subsection 146.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The appeals officer shall provide a written decision, with reasons, and a copy of any direction to the employer, employee or trade union concerned within 90 days after completing their inquiry under subsection (1). The employer shall, without delay, give a copy of it to the work place committee or health and safety representative.
The portion of section 146.2 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of a proceeding under subsection 146.1(1), an appeals officer may, subject to any regulations made under subsection (2),
Section 146.2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
The Governor in Council may make regulations, for the purpose of proceedings under subsection 146.1(1), respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the rules of practice and procedure to be followed, and the duration of the proceedings;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the setting of limits on the exercise of the powers of appeals officers under subsection (1); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any matter related to the efficient and effective operation of the proceedings.

Section.Text: Any appeals officer, as defined in subsection 122(1) of the Canada Labour Code, who is seized of an appeal under that Act immediately before the day on which section 2174 comes into force is deemed, on that day, to have been appointed as an appeals officer under subsection 145.1(1) of that Act, as enacted by section 2174, solely for the purpose of performing their duties under section 146.1 of that Act in respect of the appeal.
Sections 2172 to 2177 come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Industry, a sum not exceeding ten million dollars to the Canadian Youth Business Foundation for its use
There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Industry, a sum not exceeding seventy-five million dollars to Genome Canada for its use.

There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Human Resources and Skills Development, a sum not exceeding twenty million dollars to Pathways to Education Canada for its use.

There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Western Economic Diversification, a sum not exceeding thirteen million, five hundred thousand dollars to the Rick Hansen Foundation for its use.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 16(1) of the Telecommunications Act is replaced by the following:
A Canadian carrier is eligible to operate as a telecommunications common carrier if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is a Canadian-owned and controlled corporation incorporated or continued under the laws of Canada or a province; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it owns or operates only a transmission facility that is referred to in subsection (5).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 16(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Paragraph (1)(a) and subsections (2) and (4) do not apply in respect of the ownership or operation of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: international submarine cables;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: earth stations that provide telecommunications services by means of satellites; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: satellites.

Section.Text: The Employment Insurance Act is amended by adding the following before the heading before section 71:
There is established in the accounts of Canada an account to be known as the Employment Insurance Operating Account.
Section 71 of the Act and the heading before it are repealed.
Section 76 of the Act is repealed.
Section 80 of the Act is repealed.
The portion of paragraph 153.2(2)(a) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the making of any financial adjustments and for the crediting or charging of the amount of any of those adjustments to the Employment Insurance Operating Account, including

Section.Text: Section 158 of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 159(5) of the Act is repealed.
Section 162 of the Act is repealed.
Section 166 of the Act and the heading before it are repealed.
The Act is amended by replacing “Employment Insurance Account” with “Employment Insurance Operating Account” in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 42(2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of section 73 before paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 73.1;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: section 74;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of section 75 before paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 77(1) before paragraph (a); and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: section 78.

Section.Text: The account in the accounts of Canada known as the Employment Insurance Account is deemed to have been closed at the beginning of January 1, 2009 and removed from the accounts of Canada at that time.

For greater certainty, any amount purporting to have been credited to the Employment Insurance Account on or after January 1, 2009 and charged to the Consolidated Revenue Fund under section 76 of the Employment Insurance Act is deemed to have never been credited to that Account and charged to that Fund.

For greater certainty, all amounts credited or charged to the Employment Insurance Account on or after January 1, 2009 are deemed to have been credited or charged, as the case may be, to the Employment Insurance Operating Account established by section 70.2 of the Employment Insurance Act, as enacted by section 2185.

Every authority in any appropriation Act to make recoverable expenditures on behalf of the Employment Insurance Account in respect of the fiscal year 2010–2011 is deemed to be an authority to make recoverable expenditures on behalf of the Employment Insurance Operating Account established by section 70.2 of the Employment Insurance Act, as enacted by section 2185.

Section.MarginalNote: Employment Insurance Operating Account — audit for fiscal year 2008–2009
Section.Text: The Auditor General of Canada shall audit the Employment Insurance Operating Account, established by section 70.2 of the Employment Insurance Act, as enacted by section 2185, in respect of the fiscal year 2008–2009, and a report of that audit shall be made to the Minister of Human Resources and Skills Development.

Despite sections 2186 and 2195, and as a consequence of the establishment of the Employment Insurance Operating Account by section 70.2 of the Employment Insurance Act, as enacted by section 2185, the Auditor General of Canada may re-audit the Employment Insurance Account in respect of any fiscal year if he or she considers it necessary to do so, and a report of that audit is to be made to the Minister of Human Resources and Skills Development.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Department of Human Resources and Skills Development Act

Section.Text: Section 29 of the Department of Human Resources and Skills Development Act is replaced by the following:

The Auditor General of Canada shall annually audit the accounts and financial transactions of the Commission for the previous fiscal year. The Auditor General shall also audit the Employment Insurance Operating Account established by section 70.2 of the Employment Insurance Act, and a report of that audit shall be made to the Minister.
Section 76.08 of the Employment Insurance Regulations is replaced by the following:

The reimbursement amount paid by the Province of Quebec in accordance with section 4.5 of the Entente finale Canada-Québec sur le régime québécois d’assurance parentale, signed on March 1, 2005, shall be paid to the Receiver General and shall be considered as if it were an amount required to be paid into the Consolidated Revenue Fund under section 72 of the Act and shall be paid into that Fund.
The Regulations are amended by replacing Employment Insurance Account with Employment Insurance Operating Account in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 76.27(1)(b); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 76.27(2).

Subsection.Text: Section 127 of the Budget Implementation Act, 2008 is amended by replacing the subsections 66(1) to (3) that it enacts with the following:
Subject to subsection (7) and section 66.3, the Board shall set the premium rate for each year in order to generate just enough premium revenue during that year to ensure that at the end of that year

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the total of the amounts credited to the Employment Insurance Operating Account after December 31, 2008 is equal to the total of the amounts charged to that Account after that date; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the forecast fair market value of the Board’s reserve is equal to the amount determined under subsection (5).

Subsection.Text: Subject to any regulations made under subsections 66.1(2) and 66.2(2), the Board shall set the premium rate based on

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the information provided under sections 66.1 and 66.2;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the investment income earned by the Board;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Board’s obligation to manage a reserve, the amount of which is referred to in subsection (5);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any regulations made under section 69;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any changes, announced by the Minister on or before September 30 in a year, to payments to be made under paragraph 77(1)(a), (b) or (c) during the following year; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any other information that the Board considers relevant.

Subsection.Text: Section 127 of the Act is amended by replacing the subsection 66(5) that it enacts with the following:

The amount referred to in paragraph (1)(b) is equal to the amount of the payment made under section 70.1, indexed annually, on a compound basis, beginning in the year in which that payment is made, in accordance with the regulations.
Section 127 of the Act is amended by replacing the subsection 66.1(1) that it enacts with the following:
The Minister shall, on or before September 30 in a year, provide the Board with the following information:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the Minister has made an announcement referred to in paragraph 66(2)(e), the forecast change in the amount of the payments to be made during the following year under paragraph 77(1)(a), (b) or (c), as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the forecast costs to be paid under paragraphs 77(1)(d) and (f) during the following year, including any forecast change in those costs resulting from any change to the payments referred to in paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the total of the amounts charged to the Employment Insurance Operating Account as of the last day of the most recent month for which that total is known by the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any prescribed information.

Subsection.Text: Section 127 of the Act is amended by replacing the paragraph 66.1(2)(a) that it enacts with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing information referred to in paragraph (1)(d); and

Subsection.Text: Section 127 of the Act is amended by replacing the paragraphs 66.2(1)(b) to (f) that it enacts with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amounts forecast under subparagraphs 77.1(1)(a)(i) and (ii) and the total estimated under subparagraph 77.1(1)(a)(iii);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of any payment to be made under subsection 77.1(2) or (4) during the year; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any prescribed information.

Subsection.Text: Section 127 of the Act is amended by replacing the paragraph 66.2(2)(a) that it enacts with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing information referred to in paragraph (1)(d); and

Section.Text: Section 130 of the Act is amended by replacing the section 77.1 that it enacts with the following:

Subsection.Text: On or before September 30 in a year,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Finance shall

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: forecast the amount to be credited to the Employment Insurance Operating Account under sections 73 to 75 during the year,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: forecast the amount to be charged, during the year, to that Account under subsection 77(1) based on, among other things, information provided by the Minister, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: estimate the total, at December 31 of the immediately preceding year, of the amounts credited to that Account; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister shall estimate the total, at December 31 of the immediately preceding year, of the amounts charged to that Account.

Subsection.Text: A payment in the amount determined under subsection (3) is to be made on or before October 31 in a year to the Board out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Finance, and charged to the Employment Insurance Operating Account if

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount forecast under subparagraph (1)(a)(i);
is the amount forecast under subparagraph (1)(a)(ii);
is the total estimated under subparagraph (1)(a)(iii); and
is the total estimated under paragraph (1)(b).

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of subsection (2), the amount of the payment is an amount equal to the amount calculated in accordance with the following formula:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount forecast under subparagraph (1)(a)(i);
is the amount forecast under subparagraph (1)(a)(ii);
is the total estimated under subparagraph (1)(a)(iii); and
is the total estimated under paragraph (1)(b).

Subsection.Text: A payment in the amount determined under subsection (5) is to be made on or before October 31 in a year, or at any later date that the Minister of Finance may specify, by the Board to the Consolidated Revenue Fund and credited to the Employment Insurance Operating Account if

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount forecast under subparagraph (1)(a)(i);
is the amount forecast under subparagraph (1)(a)(ii);
is the total estimated under subparagraph (1)(a)(iii); and
is the total estimated under paragraph (1)(b).

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of subsection (4), the amount of the payment is an amount equal to the lesser of the amount of the Board’s reserve referred to in subsection 66(4) and the amount calculated in accordance with the following formula:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount forecast under subparagraph (1)(a)(i);
is the amount forecast under subparagraph (1)(a)(ii);
is the total estimated under subparagraph (1)(a)(iii); and
is the total estimated under paragraph (1)(b).

Subsection.Text: Payments under this section must be made in the manner and on the terms and conditions that the Minister of Finance may establish after consulting with the Minister and the Board.

Section.Text: Section 131 of the Act is repealed.

Heading.TitleText: Canada Employment Insurance Financing Board Act

Section.Text: Paragraph 14(3)(c) of the Canada Employment Insurance Financing Board Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a determination and analysis of any difference between the premium rate set by the Board for the year that is two years before the current year and what that rate should have been for that year in order to meet the objective of subsection 66(1) of the Employment Insurance Act; and

Subsection.Text: Sections 2185 to 2187 and 2190 to 2194 are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2009.
Section 2188 comes into force on the day on which section 127 of the Budget Implementation Act, 2008 comes into force.

Provision.Text: ---For use in the manufacture of animal feeds
---Multifilament single yarn, solely of polypropylene, textured, measuring 715 decitex or more but not exceeding 2?290 decitex, for use in the manufacture of woven fabrics
---3-thread twill weave fabrics, of polyester filaments in the warp and viscose rayon filaments in the weft, of a weight not exceeding 100 g/m², for use in the manufacture of apparel

---Plain woven fabric, from yarns of different colours, containing 70% or more by weight of polyester staple fibres in the weft, and 20% or more by weight of polyester filament yarns in the warp, of a weight not exceeding 140 g/m², for use in the manufacture of men’s and boys’ suit jackets, blazers or sports jackets

---Solely of rayon, bleached, of a width exceeding 280 cm, having a sum of yarns per 10 cm in the warp and the weft of 1,060 or more, for use in the manufacture of bed linen, duvet covers, pillow shams, cushions and cushion covers

----Solely of rayon, of a width exceeding 280 cm, having a sum of yarns per 10 cm in the warp and the weft of 1,085 or more, for use in the manufacture of bed linen, duvet covers, pillow shams, cushions and cushion covers

----Plain weave fabric, of unbleached yarns, composed predominately of viscose rayon staple fibres, mixed mainly with horsehair, cotton and polyester staple fibres, of a weight not exceeding 225 g/m², for use in the manufacture of apparel
Textile hosepiping and similar textile tubing, with or without lining, armour or accessories of other materials.

---Weft knit fabrics, solely of brushed polyester fibres, of a width exceeding 175 cm, not impregnated, coated, covered or laminated, of a weight exceeding 260 g/m² but not exceeding 290 g/m², for use in the manufacture of yarn
---Warp pile fabric, cut, solely of polyester, including the ground fabric, brushed, for use in the manufacture of coffin interiors
The proposed modification of a municipal or community building or facility for the purpose of improving energy efficiency.

The proposed modification of a municipal or community building, structure or facility for the purpose of repairing or preventing damage to property as a result of a natural disaster or a severe weather event.
The proposed installation of an intelligent transportation system and its supporting structures.
The proposed modification of a municipal or community facility for the collection, processing, diversion, treatment or disposal of solid waste.

The proposed construction, installation, operation, expansion, modification or removal of a building that is only to be used for one or more of the purposes set out in subsection (2) if the project is not to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area.
The following uses are set out for the purpose of subsection (1):

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: providing residential, institutional or other accommodations;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: providing offices, meeting rooms and related facilities;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: providing medical or emergency facilities and services;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: providing educational, financial or informational facilities and services;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: providing cultural, heritage, artistic or tourism facilities and services;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: providing recreational and sporting facilities and services;

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: providing municipal parking or maintenance facilities; and

Provision.Label: (h)
Provision.Text: presenting artistic, cultural, sporting and other community-related events.
The proposed construction, installation, operation, expansion or modification of a public transit facility,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: if the project is to be carried out within 300 m of a transportation or utility right of way or on land zoned for transportation or industrial use; and

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: is not to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area, or

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: is to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area and

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: complies with any law and any management plan, in relation to the environmentally sensitive area, and

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: in the case of an environmentally sensitive area protected by a federal government body, measures are in place to protect the area and the project has a total cost, other than the cost of land, below $10 million.
The proposed construction, installation, operation, expansion or modification of a bus rapid transit system,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: if the project is to be carried out within 300 m of a transportation or utility right of way or on land zoned for transportation or industrial use; and

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: is not to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area, or

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: is to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area and

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: complies with any law and any management plan, in relation to the environmentally sensitive area, and

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: in the case of an environmentally sensitive area protected by a federal government body, measures are in place to protect the area and the project has a total cost, other than the cost of land, below $10 million.
The proposed expansion or modification of a public transit or railway system,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: if the project is to be carried out within 300 m of a transportation or utility right of way or on land zoned for transportation or industrial use; and

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: is not to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area, or

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: is to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area and

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: complies with any law and any management plan, in relation to the environmentally sensitive area, and

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: in the case of an environmentally sensitive area protected by a federal government body, measures are in place to protect the area and the project has a total cost, other than the cost of land, below $10 million.
The proposed construction, installation, operation, expansion or modification of an overpass, a grade separation, a road intersection or a highway interchange

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: if the project is to be carried out within 300 m of a transportation or utility right of way or on land zoned for transportation or industrial use; and

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: is not to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area, or

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: is to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area and

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: complies with any law and any management plan, in relation to the environmentally sensitive area, and

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: in the case of an environmentally sensitive area protected by a federal government body, measures are in place to protect the area and the project has a total cost, other than the cost of land, below $10 million.
The proposed modification or widening of a road or public highway,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: if the project does not involve the addition of more than two lanes;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: if the project is to be carried out within 300 m of a transportation or utility right of way or on land zoned for transportation or industrial use; and

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: is not to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area, or

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: is to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area and

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: complies with any law and any management plan, in relation to the environmentally sensitive area, and

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: in the case of an environmentally sensitive area protected by a federal government body, measures are in place to protect the area and the project has a total cost, other than the cost of land, below $10 million.
The proposed modification or widening of a bridge, including the bridge approaches,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: if the project does not involve the addition of more than two lanes;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: if the project is not to be carried out in a water body; and

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: is not to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area, or

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: is to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area and

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: complies with any law and any management plan, in relation to the environmentally sensitive area, and

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: in the case of an environmentally sensitive area protected by a federal government body, measures are in place to protect the area and the project has a total cost, other than the cost of land, below $10 million.
The proposed construction, installation, operation, expansion, modification or removal of a facility for the treatment of potable water and related distribution systems,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: if the project does not involve a dam, dyke or reservoir other than a reservoir for the storage of treated water;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: if the project does not involve the extraction of ground water; and

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: is not to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area, or

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: is to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area and

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: complies with any law and any management plan, in relation to the environmentally sensitive area, and

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: in the case of an environmentally sensitive area protected by a federal government body, measures are in place to protect the area and the project has a total cost, other than the cost of land, below $10 million.
The proposed construction, installation, operation, expansion, modification or removal of a municipal or community facility for the collection or treatment of wastewater and stormwater

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: if the project is not to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area; or

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: if the project is to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area and

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: complies with any law and any management plan, in relation to the environmentally sensitive area, and

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: in the case of an environmentally sensitive area protected by a federal government body, measures are in place to protect the area and the project has a total cost, other than the cost of land, below $10 million.
The proposed construction, installation, operation, expansion or modification of an outdoor pool or rink, sports field or court, community park, recreational trail or bicycle path

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: if the project is not to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area; or

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: if the project is to be carried out within 250 m of an environmentally sensitive area and

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: complies with any law and any management plan, in relation to the environmentally sensitive area, and

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: in the case of an environmentally sensitive area protected by a federal government body, measures are in place to protect the area and the project has a total cost, other than the cost of land, below $10 million.
